pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page highlighting latest blog posts, featured books, upcoming events, and quick access to key sections.
primary_functions: Provide quick navigation to books, blog, events, reading lists, bundles, and other key sections
Highlight featured books, latest blog posts, and the next upcoming event
Offer a simple inline newsletter signup teaser that links to the full newsletter page

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a featured book card in the Featured Books section
Click on a post in the Latest Blog Posts section
Click on the upcoming event teaser 'View all events'
Click on the inline newsletter signup teaser 'Learn more' or 'Full details'

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Home'
Default landing page when visiting the site root URL

pages.name: Blog
pages.description: Listing page for all blog posts with search, filters, and sorting, including views by tag and archive year.
primary_functions: Allow users to browse, search, filter, and sort blog posts by keyword, tag, category, and year
Provide a tags and categories sidebar including options like 'Fantasy' and 'Writing Tips'
Enable users to sort posts by criteria such as newest_first or most_commented
Let users open individual blog posts in a detailed view

incoming_params.description: Optional search query string to pre-populate the blog search field (e.g., 'worldbuilding').
Blog tag code (e.g., 'fantasy', 'writing_tips') used to filter posts.
Publication year filter for posts (e.g., 2024).
Sort option value (e.g., 'newest_first', 'most_commented').
Pagination index for navigating through result pages.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a blog post title or card in the results list

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Blog'
Footer link labeled 'Blog Archive'
Accessed with query parameters to open pre-filtered views by tag, year, or sort

pages.name: Blog Post Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single blog post including saving and commenting features.
primary_functions: Display full blog post content with title, metadata, tags, and related posts
Allow users to save the post to a reading list or saved posts with a 'Save to Reading List' button
Provide a comments section with a form to submit a new comment including name, email, and message
Offer basic sharing options for the blog post

incoming_params.description: BlogPost ID whose full content should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a tag chip (e.g., 'Writing Tips', 'Fantasy') under the post title
Click on the post's year or archive link in the metadata

access_methods.description: Accessed when a blog post card is selected, using the 'id' query parameter
Can be shared externally via its URL and opened directly in the browser

pages.name: Books Catalog
pages.description: Browseable catalog of individual books with filters and sorting for series, format, price, and genre.
primary_functions: Display a list or grid of books with key information such as title, cover, series, price, and format
Provide filters for series (e.g., chronicles_of_aria, skyward_saga), format (ebook, paperback), and price range
Allow sorting of books by price, publication date (oldest_first, newest_first), or popularity
Offer quick actions on each book (such as 'Add to List' and 'Add to Wishlist') and links to the book detail page

incoming_params.description: Series code (e.g., 'chronicles_of_aria', 'skyward_saga') to pre-filter the catalog.
Book format filter value (e.g., 'ebook', 'paperback').
Minimum price filter; optional and usually left blank.
Maximum price filter for price-sensitive browsing (e.g., 6 for ebooks under $6).
Primary genre filter such as 'fantasy' or 'science_fiction'.
Sort option for the catalog (e.g., 'price_asc', 'publication_oldest_first').
Pagination index for navigating catalog pages.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a book title or cover image in the catalog grid
After using 'Add to List', click 'View this list' in the confirmation message
Click 'View Cart' after adding a book to the cart from the catalog

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Books'
Accessed with query parameters to open a pre-filtered or pre-sorted catalog view

pages.name: Book Detail
pages.description: Detailed information page for a single book, including series position, pricing, and actions.
primary_functions: Show detailed information about a specific book including description, series, price, formats, and metadata
Allow users to add the book to their wishlist with an 'Add to Wishlist' button
Allow users to add the book to an existing reading list via an 'Add to List' or 'Save to Reading List' control
Provide an option to add the book to the shopping cart and navigate to related books

incoming_params.description: Book ID for which details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on the series name to view all books in that series
After adding to a reading list, click 'View this list'
Click 'View Wishlist' after adding the book to the wishlist
Click 'View Cart' after adding the book to the cart

access_methods.description: Reached when a specific book is selected, using the 'id' query parameter
Can be linked directly from external pages or marketing campaigns

pages.name: Reading Lists
pages.description: Management page for creating and viewing custom reading lists.
primary_functions: Display all user-created reading lists with options to select, rename, or delete a list
Allow users to create a new reading list by specifying a list name such as 'Spring Reading Plan'
When a specific list is selected (e.g., via a listId parameter), show its contents in order with options to reorder or remove items
Provide entry points to open book detail pages for items in a reading list

incoming_params.description: ID of a specific reading list to pre-select and display its contents.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Add books from this series' or a similar CTA inside a list
Click on a book title or cover within a reading list

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Reading Lists'
Footer link labeled 'Reading Lists'
Supports deep-linking directly to a specific reading list via the 'listId' parameter

pages.name: Wishlist
pages.description: Page for viewing and managing wishlist items.
primary_functions: Display all books that the user has added to their wishlist
Allow users to remove items from the wishlist
Provide options to move or copy wishlist items to a reading list or to the shopping cart
Link to book detail pages for more information on each wishlist item

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a wishlist item title or cover image
After moving an item to a reading list, click 'View list'
Click 'View Cart' after adding a wishlist item to the cart

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Wishlist'
Footer link labeled 'Wishlist'

pages.name: Events
pages.description: Listing page for all upcoming events with filters and date selection.
primary_functions: Display upcoming events with key details such as title, type, date, and time
Provide filters for event type (online, in_person) and other attributes if needed
Allow users to select a date range or month (e.g., the next calendar month) to filter events
Enable users to open an event detail page for more information and registration

incoming_params.description: Event type filter value such as 'online' or 'in_person'.
Shortcut month option value used to set a date range (e.g., next calendar month).
Custom start date (ISO string) for date range filtering.
Custom end date (ISO string) for date range filtering.
Optional text search term to match in event titles and descriptions (e.g., 'Live Q&A').
Pagination index for navigating through events.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an event title or card in the events list

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Events'

pages.name: Event Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single event, including registration and saving options.
primary_functions: Show full details about a specific event including description, format, and schedule
Display available sessions or time slots and allow users to select a suitable time (e.g., starting at or after 6_00_pm)
Provide a registration form to capture attendee information such as name and email
After successful registration, show a confirmation state with an option to add or save the event to the My Events list

incoming_params.description: Event ID whose details and sessions should be displayed.
Optional pre-selected event session ID, used when navigating from My Events.
Optional registration ID to highlight the user's registration details.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Go to My Events' after adding or registering for an event
Click 'Back to all events'

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Events list using the 'id' query parameter
Can be shared externally via direct event URLs

pages.name: My Events
pages.description: Overview of events the user has registered for or saved.
primary_functions: List all events that the user has added to their My Events collection
Allow users to open the full event detail page for any saved event
Provide basic management actions such as removing an event from My Events

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an event in the My Events list to view or manage details
Click 'Browse all events' from the My Events page

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'My Events'
Footer link labeled 'My Events'

pages.name: Quotes
pages.description: Listing page for notable quotes from the author's books.
primary_functions: Display a collection of quote cards including text, book source, and like counts
Provide a book filter dropdown to limit quotes to a specific book such as 'the_moonlit_archive'
Allow sorting of quotes by criteria such as most_liked or newest
Enable users to open a quote detail page for a selected quote

incoming_params.description: Book ID used to filter quotes to a specific book (e.g., 'The Moonlit Archive').
Sort option value such as 'most_liked' or 'newest'.
Pagination index for navigating through quotes.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a quote card to see full details and sharing options
Click 'View book' from a quote card

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Quotes' (may appear under an 'Extras' menu in UI)

pages.name: Quote Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single quote with sharing capability.
primary_functions: Display an individual quote in full with book information and any relevant context
Provide a 'Copy Share Link' or 'Get Link' function that shows or copies a shareable URL for the quote
Allow users to like or favorite the quote
Offer navigation back to the main quotes listing

incoming_params.description: Quote ID whose details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to all quotes from this book'
Click on the book title in the quote detail view

access_methods.description: Accessed by selecting a quote from the Quotes list, using the 'id' query parameter
Shareable URL opened directly when a copied share link is visited

pages.name: Bundles
pages.description: Shop listing for book bundles with filtering for digital bundles and price.
primary_functions: Display available book bundles with key details such as title, included items count, format, and price
Provide filters for bundle price range (including a maximum price) and format (ebook or digital_only)
Offer a filter for the number of books in a bundle (e.g., 4_or_more)
Allow users to open a bundle detail page for the bundle they are interested in

incoming_params.description: Bundle format filter such as 'ebook' or 'digital_only'.
Minimum bundle price filter; optional and typically left blank.
Maximum bundle price filter (e.g., 25 for bundles under $25).
Minimum number of books included in a bundle (e.g., 4 for '4 or more').
Sort option value for bundles (e.g., 'price_asc').
Pagination index for navigating bundle results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a bundle title or image in the bundles list

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Bundles' (may appear under a 'Shop' menu in UI)
Footer link labeled 'Shop Bundles'
Supports pre-filtered bundle views via query parameters

pages.name: Bundle Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific bundle with cart integration.
primary_functions: Show the full contents of a bundle including a list of included books and formats
Display pricing, format information, and any discounts for the bundle
Allow users to confirm quantity (typically 1) and add the bundle to the shopping cart

incoming_params.description: Bundle ID whose details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View Cart' after adding the bundle to the cart
Click 'Back to bundles'
Click on an included book within the bundle contents list

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Bundles list via the 'id' query parameter

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Shopping cart page summarizing items selected for purchase.
primary_functions: Display all items currently in the cart including bundles and individual books with quantities and prices
Allow users to update item quantities or remove items from the cart
Provide an estimated total and a path toward checkout or a placeholder explaining how purchases are handled

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Continue shopping for books'
Click 'Continue shopping for bundles'

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Cart'

pages.name: Newsletter
pages.description: Dedicated page for subscribing to the author newsletter.
primary_functions: Present a newsletter signup form with fields for name and email
Allow users to choose an update_frequency (e.g., monthly) from a dropdown
Provide a list of genre checkboxes including fantasy, science_fiction, and other genres that can be checked or unchecked
Include options such as 'include blog updates' that can be toggled, and submit the subscription while displaying a confirmation message

incoming_params.description: Optional tracking parameter indicating where the user came from (e.g., 'home_teaser', 'footer').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to Home' or 'Start exploring' link after successful subscription

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Newsletter'
Footer link labeled 'Newsletter'
Accessed when user clicks through from inline newsletter prompts

pages.name: About
pages.description: Information about the author and basic site information, including contact and legal sections.
primary_functions: Provide background information about the author, their books, and the purpose of the website
Offer contact information or a simple contact form so readers can reach out
Summarize basic privacy and terms information or link to more detailed policies via sections on the same page

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to Home' or site logo from the About page

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'About'
Footer link labeled 'About the Author'
Anchored links 'Contact' (#contact) and 'Privacy & Terms' (#policies) from the footer

header_links.description: Homepage with featured books, latest blog posts, and upcoming events
Browse all individual books by series, format, and price
Author blog with posts on writing, worldbuilding, and news
Upcoming author events and live sessions
Create and manage your custom reading lists
Shop book bundles (accessible via the Shop dropdown in the main navigation)
Memorable book quotes (accessible via the Extras menu)
View and manage books saved to your wishlist
View events you have registered for or saved
Subscribe to email updates with preferred genres and frequency
View items currently in your shopping cart
About the author, contact information, and site details

footer_links.description: Learn more about the author and the purpose of this site
Contact information or a form to send a message to the author
Go to the full newsletter signup page
Browse the full archive of blog posts
Access and manage your reading lists
View and adjust books in your wishlist
See all events you have registered for or saved
Browse available book bundles for purchase
Summary of privacy practices and terms of use
